summary: CASE-Lingxi has a coherent evidence-backed thesis: conservative static production menus currently dominate tested adaptive routers, and agents should remain in the research/audit layer until OOS promotion gates are passed. The ARA is structurally complete, claims are falsifiable, and negative paths are preserved. A first paired bootstrap promotion audit has now been added. The main remaining weaknesses are broader statistical testing, capacity/slippage/data-bias constraints, and submission-grade bibliographic detail.

dimensions.name: Evidence Relevance
strengths: Every core claim points to a named experiment ID.
Router, RL, tag, and meta-selector claims cite exact summary artifacts and win counts.
Evidence includes both positive production-menu support and negative adaptation results.
weaknesses: The production-menu claim relies on a benchmark master table plus reports rather than a single unified promotion-test manifest.
The new promotion audit adds paired daily-return bootstrap intervals for the main adaptive candidates, but not yet for every older Lingxi/SOTA comparison.
suggestions: Extend the promotion audit to earlier sparse-regime, market-regime, and SOTA-proxy candidates.
Add drawdown and Sharpe bootstrap intervals, not only annualized return difference intervals.

dimensions.name: Falsifiability Quality
strengths: Claims include clear falsification criteria such as future OOS routers beating the static menu.
Production versus research-only labels are testable against committed scripts and artifacts.
weaknesses: Production thresholds are now numeric, but future-falsification criteria still need minimum OOS window length and market coverage rules.
suggestions: Separate production gate criteria for return-seeking and risk-control sleeves.

dimensions.name: Scope Calibration
strengths: The artifact repeatedly limits claims to current evidence.
RL and LLM-tag methods are explicitly labeled research-only or negative controls.
Constraints mention static universe bias, proxy implementations, and simplified transaction costs.
weaknesses: The abstract-level claim that the static menu is supported across A-share, US, HK, and crypto could be misread as live-trading readiness.
Capacity, borrow, liquidity, and real execution constraints remain underdeveloped.
suggestions: Add a trading-readiness disclaimer to the paper introduction and ARA constraints.
Add a capacity/slippage stress-test plan before any deployment claim.

dimensions.name: Argument Coherence
strengths: Problem, method, claims, experiments, and evidence form a coherent chain.
The validation echo trap provides a clear bridge from empirical results to theoretical framing.
The paper draft and ARA use the same conceptual vocabulary.
weaknesses: The theory section would benefit from a tighter formal statement of promotion gates and research-agent policy.
Related work is currently local-source based and needs submission-grade citation detail.
suggestions: Move the formalization in paper/formulation.md into the ARA logic layer or cross-link it.
Add BibTeX-backed citations for the final manuscript.

dimensions.name: Exploration Integrity
strengths: The trace records real rejected paths including validation-only meta-selection.
Negative router, RL, and market-tag results are preserved rather than hidden.
The artifact distinguishes production, research-only, and rejected states.
weaknesses: The trace is concise and does not yet include every prior router variant such as sparse regime and market-regime routers.
Some decisions are summarized after the fact rather than linked to full chronological logs.
suggestions: Expand exploration_tree.yaml with sparse regime, market-regime, SOTA proxy, and PITNorm-gate branches.
Add provenance tags for user-suggested, AI-suggested, and AI-executed events.

dimensions.name: Methodological Rigor
strengths: Experiments use explicit scripts, row counts, costs, OOS windows, and comparable scenario matrices.
A unified promotion audit now adds paired daily-return block bootstrap intervals for the main adaptive candidates.
The RL router freezes training before OOS evaluation.
LLM-compatible tags are generated with a leakage-aware point-in-time schema.
weaknesses: Bootstrap confidence intervals currently cover annualized daily-return differences only for the main adaptive candidates.
No multiple-comparison correction is applied despite many scenarios and candidates.
Execution realism is limited to a fixed 10 bps cost and does not include market impact or capacity.
The LLM-tag experiment uses deterministic proxy tags rather than actual timestamped LLM debate outputs.
suggestions: Extend paired bootstrap tests to Sharpe, drawdown, and earlier candidate families.
Add sensitivity grids for transaction cost, TopK, and OOS window definitions.
Add a future experiment with frozen timestamped LLM debate tags while preserving the same schema.
Add capacity and liquidity diagnostics for production-facing claims.

findings.evidence_span: E06 adds block-bootstrap intervals for candidate-minus-menu daily returns.
findings.observation: The main router conclusions now include paired annualized daily-return bootstrap intervals, but the audit does not yet cover Sharpe or drawdown intervals.
findings.reasoning: Annualized return intervals improve rigor, but promotion decisions also depend on Sharpe and drawdown.
findings.suggestion: Add paired bootstrap confidence intervals for Sharpe and drawdown differences.

findings.evidence_span: A future OOS experiment shows a dynamic router beating the static menu across the required market/TopK/variant matrix under transaction costs.
findings.observation: Falsification criteria are meaningful but not fully parameterized.
findings.reasoning: A reviewer cannot tell the exact OOS length, number of markets, or metric thresholds required for promotion.
findings.suggestion: Define a numeric promotion gate with minimum OOS days, minimum scenario win rate, and maximum drawdown degradation.

findings.evidence_span: Some global-market datasets use static large-cap universes and may include survivorship bias.
findings.observation: Data limitations are acknowledged but not quantified.
findings.reasoning: Static universe and survivorship bias can materially affect cross-market strategy results.
findings.suggestion: Add a data-bias appendix with universe construction, delisting/survivorship risks, and sensitivity to alternate universes.

findings.evidence_span: Trace includes context router, RL router, market-tag ablation, and validation-only selector.
findings.observation: The trace captures the main current branches but omits several prior experiments that shaped the final conclusion.
findings.reasoning: Adding sparse regime, market-regime, adaptive router, and PITNorm-gate branches would improve research process recoverability.
findings.suggestion: Expand the trace with prior router and SOTA-proxy branches linked to their reports.

findings.evidence_span: Local evidence paths are listed instead of full bibliographic citations.
findings.observation: The paper-level related work is not yet submission-grade.
findings.reasoning: The local references are useful for repository navigation, but a manuscript needs complete citations and venue/status verification.
findings.suggestion: Add BibTeX-backed citations and separate CCF-A/venue-verified claims from arXiv/preprint claims.

next_actions: Extend paired bootstrap tests to Sharpe and drawdown differences.
Extend the unified CASE promotion audit to earlier sparse-regime, market-regime, and SOTA-proxy candidates.
Expand ARA trace to include earlier router and PITNorm/SOTA branches.
Upgrade paper related work with BibTeX-backed citations.
